---
title: "5.3.2 Keeping data safe from security threats"
subtitle: "Email and URL checks, firewalls and proxy servers · P205-P208"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        .reveal .term { color: #cf1f2e; font-weight: 700; }
        .reveal .mark { background: #fff36d; padding: 0 0.08em; }
        .reveal .tight-list li { margin-bottom: 0.24em; }
        .reveal .figure-note { font-size: 0.82em; line-height: 1.28; }
        .reveal .figure-placeholder { border: 1px dashed #8aa0bf; border-radius: 8px; background: #f4f8ff; color: #40526b; padding: 1.1em 1.3em; margin: 0.8em 0; font-size: 0.9em; text-align: center; }
        .reveal .clean-table table { font-size: 0.68em; line-height: 1.06; }
        .reveal .clean-table th, .reveal .clean-table td { padding: 0.2em 0.3em; vertical-align: top; }
        </style>
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 5 Directory / 返回 Unit 5 目录](../../index.html)

## Email Checks

### Before opening links

When emails are sent to you, there are actions to take before opening them or activating links in them.

::: {.tight-list}
- check the spellings in the email and in the links
- professional, genuine organisations will not send emails with spelling or major grammatical errors
- carefully check the tone used in the email message
- if it rushes you or the language is inappropriate, it could be phishing or worse
:::

## Five Things to Look Out For

### Email address and tone

::: {.tight-list}
1. Check the email address itself; no legitimate company will use an address such as `@gmail.com`.
2. The tone of the email and bad spelling of words is a clear indication of a potential scam.
:::

::: {.figure-placeholder}
Figure 5.19 Sample scam email screenshot placeholder
:::

## Typo Squatting

### Misspelled domain names

Misspelling of domain names in a link is very common in emails sent by scammers and fraudsters.

Textbook examples:

```text
www.gougle.com
www.amozon.com
```

This is known as <span class="term">typo squatting</span>.

## Suspicious Links

### Destination address mismatch

Destination addresses should match the rest of the email.

::: {.figure-placeholder}
Figure 5.20 Second example of probable scam screenshot placeholder
:::

Other errors include insecure `http`, incorrect company spelling and unexpected domain types.

## Firewalls

### Filtering traffic

A <span class="term">firewall</span> can be either software or hardware.

It sits between the user's computer and an external network, for example the internet, and filters information in and out of the computer.

::: {.figure-placeholder}
Figure 5.21 Typical firewall setup screenshot placeholder
:::

## Firewall Tasks

### Traffic checks

Main firewall tasks include:

::: {.tight-list}
- examine traffic between a user's computer/internal network and a public network
- check whether incoming or outgoing data meets criteria
- block traffic and warn the user or network manager if criteria fail
- log incoming and outgoing traffic for later interrogation
- prevent access to undesirable sites using a list of undesirable IP addresses
:::

## Firewall Protection

### Viruses, hackers and software access

Firewalls can help prevent viruses or hackers entering the user's computer or internal network.

The user is warned if software on their system is trying to access an external data source and can allow or deny the access.

Firewalls can be hardware interfaces or software installed on a computer.

## Firewall Limits

### What firewalls cannot control

Firewalls cannot prevent:

::: {.tight-list}
- internal users using their own hardware devices to bypass the firewall
- employee misconduct or carelessness, such as poor password or user account control
- users on stand-alone computers disabling the firewall
:::

These issues require management control or personal control.

## Proxy Servers

### Intermediate server

<span class="term">Proxy servers</span> act as an intermediate between the user and a web server.

::: {.figure-placeholder}
Figure 5.22 Proxy server screenshot placeholder
:::

## Proxy Server Features

### Filtering and protection

Proxy servers:

::: {.tight-list}
- allow internet traffic to be filtered and websites to be blocked
- keep users' IP addresses secret
- allow valid internet traffic and deny invalid traffic
- block requests from certain IP addresses
- prevent direct access to a web server
- can act as firewalls
:::

## Proxy Server Features

### Attack handling and cache

If an attack is launched, it hits the proxy server instead, helping prevent hacking and DoS attacks.

Proxy servers can direct invalid traffic away from web servers.

Using a <span class="term">cache</span>, the proxy server can speed up access to information from a website by storing the home page after the first visit.

## Classroom Check

### Email checks and firewalls

A complete answer should include:

::: {.tight-list}
- scam clues include spelling errors, odd tone, suspicious email domains, typo-squatting and mismatched links
- a firewall filters information between a computer/internal network and an external network
- firewalls can block traffic, warn users, log traffic and restrict undesirable sites
- firewalls cannot stop misconduct, bypass devices or users disabling protection
:::

## Classroom Check

### Proxy servers

A complete answer should include:

::: {.tight-list}
- proxy servers sit between user and web server, filter traffic and hide IP addresses
- proxy servers can use cache and absorb attacks before they reach the web server
:::

## Bridge

### Next: Privacy settings and SSL

The next deck closes Unit 5 with privacy controls and secure sockets layer.

## End

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 5 Directory / 返回 Unit 5 目录](../../index.html)
